sentence_reviews.text: Vision–language–action models map visual observations and language instructions to temporally grounded actions or action tokens for embodied control.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RT-2 describes vision-language-action models that co-fine-tune web-scale vision-language models and robotics data, representing robot actions as tokens for robotic control.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited RT-2 source directly supports the vision/language-to-action-token framing.

sentence_reviews.text: They connect foundation-model perception and instruction following directly to physical or simulated behavior.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RT-2 connects vision-language model knowledge to robotic control; the sim-to-real survey supports embodied transfer challenges.
sentence_reviews.notes: The exact wording is synthesized, but the core link from perception/instructions to behavior is supported.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RT-2 evaluates robotic control/manipulation tasks using VLA-style action tokens.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the cited robotics VLA source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: ReAct covers language-model reasoning and acting, while RT-2 covers VLA action prediction for robots; neither directly verifies screen-action prediction.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources do not directly support screen-action prediction as a VLA example.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support embodied AI and robotic control broadly but do not directly establish navigation from language as an example.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible within embodied VLA systems, but the exact example needs a navigation-specific citation.

sentence_reviews.text: Action data are expensive, embodiment shifts are severe, and mistakes can create real physical consequences.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RT-2 motivates scarce robot data and transfer from web-scale knowledge; the sim-to-real survey covers embodiment and transfer gaps in robotics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation is well supported by synthesis, though the physical-consequence wording is general robotics safety reasoning.

sentence_reviews.text: Vision–language–action models extends or specializes the architecture family Multimodal foundation models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RT-2 transfers web-scale vision-language model knowledge to robotic control.
sentence_reviews.notes: The relationship is supported for RT-2-like systems, though 'multimodal foundation models' is atlas taxonomy wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Vision–language–action models extends or specializes the architecture family Latent world models & predictive dynamics.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer supports world-model predictive dynamics; RT-2 supports VLA robotics, but RT-2 is not presented as a latent world model.
sentence_reviews.notes: Some embodied agents may use world models, but this dependency is not directly established for VLA models generally.

sentence_reviews.text: Vision–language–action models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto supports policy-gradient and actor-critic methods; RT-2 is a VLA robotics model but is not primarily described as actor-critic training.
sentence_reviews.notes: Actor-critic methods are relevant to robotics policies but not directly supported as a common VLA objective here.

sentence_reviews.text: Vision–language–action models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RT-2 action-token formulation and robotic control tasks support temporally ordered action outputs.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sequence/time representation claim is defensible for action-token policies.

edge_reviews.notes: RT-2 supports the multimodal foundation-model lineage for VLA models.
World models are relevant to embodied control but are not shown as a common component of VLA models by the cited sources.
Policy-gradient/actor-critic methods are relevant background but not established as a common VLA optimization dependency.
Action-token and embodied-control setup supports temporal sequence representation.
